title: Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner
exclusions: Does NOT judge the quality of the certification (rubber-stamp vs diligent); follows up with sampling review.
evidence: Daily report 'access_review_freshness' writes review-age per control owner to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AC-2(j) requires review of accounts at least annually; the UC measures compliance continuously.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AC-2 (Account management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.3: Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.18 access rights — periodic review is a core requirement.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.5.18 (Access rights review) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.3: Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX ITGC periodic access review is a cornerstone audit test.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX ITGC ITGC.AccessMgmt.Review (Periodic access review) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.3: Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a control owner has not completed an access review for a scoped application in 90+ days, the UC fires with the owner, app_id, and last_review_at.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Applications with reviews certified within the 90-day window do NOT fire.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=iam:access_review earliest=-365d
| stats max(_time) as last_review by app_id reviewer
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_review)/86400,0)
| where days_since>90
| lookup app_inventory.csv app_id OUTPUT owner criticality
| table owner app_id criticality last_review days_since
description: Tracks the age of the most recent access review per application and control owner. Applications falling outside the 90-day cadence drive follow-up tickets.
value: Access review is the #1 ITGC deficiency in typical public-company audits; turning it into a green/red KPI changes the operating rhythm.
implementation: (1) Ingest review-completion events from the GRC tool; (2) refresh app_inventory.csv weekly; (3) run daily.
visualization: Heatmap of owner × application age, table of overdue, single-value '% of apps reviewed within 90 days'.
knownFalsePositives: New applications added in-cycle need a baseline review; track onboarding date.
grandmaExplanation: Access review is the #1 ITGC deficiency in typical public-company audits; turning it into a green/red key numbers changes the operating rhythm — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest review-completion events from the GRC tool; (2) refresh app_inventory.csv weekly; (3) run daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=iam:access_review earliest=-365d
| stats max(_time) as last_review by app_id reviewer
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_review)/86400,0)
| where days_since>90
| lookup app_inventory.csv app_id OUTPUT owner criticality
| table owner app_id criticality last_review days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner** — Access review is the #1 ITGC deficiency in typical public-company audits; turning it into a green/red KPI changes the operating rhythm.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: iam:access_review. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=iam:access_review, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_id reviewer** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since>90` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Pipeline stage (see **Periodic access review SLA — stale certifications by control owner**): table owner app_id criticality last_review days_since


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of owner × application age, table of overdue, single-value '% of apps reviewed within 90 days'.
